markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both DN SOOPers and Nongshim Red Force each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing indirect negotiations between the United States and Iran, mediated by Pakistan, continue under a fragile extended ceasefire that began in April 2026. Recent exchanges of peace proposals have focused on ending the conflict, reopening the Strait of Hormuz, lifting U.S. sanctions, and addressing Iran's nuclear program through limits on enrichment and stockpile handover. President Trump has rejected Iran's latest counteroffer as unacceptable while warning of renewed military action if progress stalls, and Iran has conveyed further revisions without committing to immediate nuclear talks. These developments, alongside new U.S. sanctions on Iranian oil exports, highlight persistent gaps in trust and core demands that shape trader assessments of whether a permanent agreement can be finalized by year-end.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) hit (HIGH) $120 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy approaches this Exort Series Main Stage BO3 with the clear edge in recent European CS2 form, having posted an undefeated Swiss stage record built on disciplined map control and academy-level coordination backed by Team Spirit's infrastructure. Wave Esports enters after a winless group run marked by inconsistent execution across multiple maps and limited depth in their current roster, leaving them exposed against opponents who rotate young prospects effectively. Head-to-head trends in comparable C-tier events favor academy squads with stronger map pools and recent practice emphasis, while the online format eliminates travel variables but keeps individual player availability and last-minute lineup tweaks as the primary swing factors for series outcomes.

events.title: Villarreal CF vs. Club Atlético de Madrid
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for Sunday, May 24, 2026 between Villarreal CF and Club Atlético de Madrid.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both teams enter this La Liga season finale at Estadio de la Cerámica with nearly identical records and points tallies, creating a tight three-way market split. Villarreal’s strong home form and recent surge that secured Champions League qualification stand against Atlético Madrid’s defensive organization and squad depth, though the visitors face a key suspension for Robin Le Normand and potential rotation after earlier European commitments. Head-to-head results favor Atlético from their earlier 2-0 win, yet the Yellow Submarine’s attacking momentum at home narrows the gap. Coach Marcelino’s impending departure adds motivation for Villarreal, while both sides’ comparable recent results and table positioning keep the implied probabilities clustered and the outcome highly uncertain.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CO-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CO-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polling in Dublin Central indicates Sinn Féin’s Janice Boylan holds a narrow first-preference lead, yet traders price Daniel Ennis of the Social Democrats at 74 percent as the likely winner. Under Ireland’s single transferable vote system, Ennis benefits from strong second- and later-preference flows from centre-left and independent voters once lower-polling candidates are eliminated. Gerry Hutch’s independent candidacy, at 5.5 percent, draws limited first preferences but could redirect support toward Ennis rather than Boylan in subsequent counts. The May 22 vote occurs amid weak performances by government parties, with Fine Gael and Fianna Fáil candidates trailing and little movement reported in the final campaign week.

markets.question: Will Monza achieve promotion from Serie B to Serie A for the 2026-27 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team achieves promotion from Serie B to Serie A for the 2026–27 season at the conclusion of the 2025-2026 league season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in Serie A for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Serie B, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by Serie A and Serie B.

If the 2025-2026 Serie B season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from Serie A and Serie B. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the MN-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MN-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump’s repeated public statements questioning NATO’s value and threatening U.S. withdrawal, coupled with the May 2026 Pentagon order to pull 5,000 troops from Germany amid tensions over the Iran conflict, have shaped trader views on near-term exit probabilities. These moves occur against a 2023 congressional statute requiring two-thirds Senate approval or an act of Congress before the United States can suspend, terminate, or withdraw from the North Atlantic Treaty. No formal notification process or legislative debate has begun, while European allies accelerate independent defense planning ahead of the July 2026 NATO summit in Ankara. Market-implied odds remain low because any formal denunciation would face immediate institutional and legal constraints that have not yet been tested.

markets.question: Will the U.S. flu hospitalization rate per 100,000 in Week 19 be between 90 and 95?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the cumulative influenza-associated hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the United States, as reported for the specified week.

If the recorded data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be CDC FluView / FluSurv-NET (see: https://www.cdc.gov/fluview/index.html). If the FluSurv-NET hospitalization rate for the specified week is not released by 11:59 PM ET on the tenth calendar day following the date of the prior FluView weekly report release, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

Note: Only the CDC FluSurv-NET cumulative hospitalization rate per 100,000 population for the specified week will qualify, regardless of estimates, projections, state-level reports, or other influenza surveillance metrics published by the CDC or other sources.

events.title: Flu Hospitalization Rate Week 19, 2026?

If the FluSurv-NET hospitalization rate for the specified week is not released by 11:59 PM ET on the tenth calendar day following the date of the prior FluView weekly report release, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent CDC FluView data confirm low seasonal influenza activity through Week 18, with weekly hospitalization rates below 1 per 100,000 and a cumulative rate of 84.5 per 100,000 by mid-April that has advanced only modestly since. Influenza A(H3N2) remains the dominant strain overall, yet transmission has declined sharply in late spring, consistent with historical patterns for moderate seasons where cumulative FluSurv-NET rates stabilize near 85 per 100,000. This trajectory underpins the market’s overwhelming 99.4% consensus on the 85–90 band. An unexpected late-season resurgence or substantial upward revision in pending Week 19 reports could still shift the final figure.

markets.question: Will MiniMax have the best AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank among primarily Chinese companies, based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Qualifying Chinese Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Meituan” would be ranked ahead of “Xiaomi”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alibaba and Baidu remain locked in a tight contest for the top spot, driven by their parallel advances in agentic AI capabilities and enterprise cloud offerings. Both companies have accelerated model integrations and infrastructure builds in recent weeks, with Alibaba highlighting Qwen ecosystem growth and a restructured Token Hub for streamlined AI deployment, while Baidu unveiled DuMate and Miaoda agents at its May Create 2026 conference alongside new Daily Active Agents metrics. This back-and-forth momentum in agent performance, multimodal features, and domestic chip utilization sustains the narrow trader consensus. Upcoming benchmark releases, enterprise adoption figures, or further agent platform expansions before month-end could widen the gap by demonstrating clearer leadership in practical AI scaling.

markets.question: Will "The Proposal" be the top global Netflix movie this week?
markets.description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV movies list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, May 19, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which movie this update ranks as the #1 global Netflix movie.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for Global Top 10 Movies (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by May 22, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Swapped commands overwhelming trader consensus as the top global Netflix movie this week, driven by its commanding position in recent streaming metrics and sustained viewer hours viewed since launch. Strong critical reception, effective promotional campaigns, and favorable platform placement have reinforced its frontrunner status, mirroring historical patterns where new releases with broad appeal quickly dominate weekly charts. While the market-implied odds reflect this decisive momentum, an upset remains possible if a competing title experiences a late surge in engagement or if Netflix’s final weekly data reveals unexpected shifts in regional performance before the period closes.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the Maine Senate race in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 midterm Maine U.S. Senate election, inclusive of any run-offs.

A candidate shall be considered to represent a party in the event that he or she is the nominee of the party in question. Candidates other than the Democratic or Republican nominee (e.g., Greens, Libertarian, independent) may be added at a later date.

Candidates who run as independents will not be encompassed by the “Democrat” or “Republican” options regardless of any affiliation they may have with the party.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race in this state for the same candidate, this market will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polling in the Maine Senate race shows Democratic challenger Graham Platner maintaining a consistent lead over incumbent Republican Susan Collins ahead of the November 2026 general election. Platner’s commanding position in the June 9 Democratic primary, following Governor Janet Mills’ withdrawal, has strengthened the party’s general-election prospects in a state that has trended Democratic in recent cycles. Traders appear to weigh these head-to-head survey results and the midterm environment against Collins’s long record of narrow victories, producing the current 74 percent implied probability for a Democratic winner. Key factors that could still shift sentiment include primary turnout, any late endorsements, and broader national political conditions through the fall.

markets.question: Will Marco Rubio win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

events.title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
events.description:

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Early positioning for the 2028 presidential race reflects an open field without an incumbent, with trader consensus centering on Vice President JD Vance at the top due to his administration role and conservative base support, followed closely by California Governor Gavin Newsom and Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Recent developments include Newsom’s October 2025 confirmation that he will weigh a Democratic bid after the 2026 midterms, alongside memoir releases and early-state visibility that have sustained his profile. Vance has faced modest polling softness amid foreign-policy negotiations, while Rubio has gained ground through cabinet visibility and aligned policy stances. The 2026 midterms and any major party endorsements or primary polling shifts could widen gaps among these and other listed contenders before formal nominations begin.

markets.question: Real Madrid CF vs. Athletic Club: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Real Madrid CF and Athletic Club, scheduled for May 23 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Real Madrid CF and Athletic Club combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Real Madrid CF vs. Athletic Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 23 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Madrid enters its home La Liga clash against Athletic Club on May 23 or 24 amid a crippling injury crisis that has sidelined stars like Kylian Mbappé (hamstring), Federico Valverde (head injury from training altercation), Rodrygo, Dani Carvajal, Éder Militão, Dani Ceballos, and Arda Güler, leaving the squad depleted after a recent El Clásico defeat on May 10 that dimmed title hopes in a tight standings race where they trail Barcelona. Athletic Club, pushing for a top-four finish, faces its own absences including Nico Williams (hamstring) and Oihan Sancet (hamstring), both potentially out until late May, while Beñat Prados recovers from a cruciate ligament tear. Madrid's home advantage and historical dominance in head-to-heads (24 wins in last 34) contrast with fatigue from a congested schedule featuring Copa del Rey and Sevilla away beforehand, heightening uncertainty for traders.

markets.question: Will ELAM win the most seats at the Cyprus House of Representatives election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Cyprus on May 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Cyprus House of Representatives.

If voting in the Cyprus House of Representatives election does not occur by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between this party and any other for the most seats won, this market will be resolved in favor of the party which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Central Electoral Service of the Ministry of Interior of the Republic of Cyprus (https://elections.gov.cy).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polling trends position DISY as the frontrunner to secure the largest share of seats in Cyprus's May 24 House of Representatives election under the proportional representation system. Surveys conducted in early May show DISY holding a narrow lead over AKEL, with both parties ahead of rising support for ELAM and the emergence of newcomer ALMA. A record 753 candidates across more than 15 parties signals likely fragmentation that could dilute the combined strength of centrist groups backing President Nikos Christodoulides, including DIKO, DIPA, and EDEK. Traders' consensus on these probabilities reflects the latest voting intention data and the limited time remaining before results are expected shortly after polls close.

markets.question: Will Lando Norris win the 2026 Action of the Year?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the driver who wins the 2026 Action of the Year award for the 2026 Formula 1 season as awarded at the FIA Awards.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by FIA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 Formula 1 season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the FIA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli’s record-setting start to the 2026 Formula 1 season has shaped trader consensus around him for Action of the Year. The Mercedes rookie secured three consecutive pole-to-win results across the Chinese, Japanese, and Miami Grands Prix, extending his Drivers’ Championship lead to 20 points while becoming the first driver to achieve that pole-to-victory streak in his opening races. His composed racecraft under pressure from McLaren’s Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri in Miami, combined with historical parallels to Ayrton Senna, has positioned these performances as the early benchmark for memorable action. Max Verstappen’s lap-one recovery in Miami and isolated standout overtakes by Franco Colapinto or others trail significantly, reflecting the market’s focus on sustained dominance over single moments.
